reasoning:
As of May 11, 2025, Bitcoin is trading around $103,500 - $104,500, which is roughly 11% above the target close price of $93,429.20. While an 11% premium might seem substantial, it offers a relatively thin margin of safety over a 7.5-month horizon for an asset with an implied volatility near 60%. Due to the variance drag (the tendency for the median of a log-normal distribution to fall below its mean over time), standard option pricing models (like Black-Scholes) assign only a ~52-54% risk-neutral probability of Bitcoin closing above $93.4k starting from $104k. 

Furthermore, the Metaculus community prediction was 42% on May 1 (when the price was around $95,700). Applying the same implied volatility and drift models used by the community on May 1 to today's price of $104,000 suggests a mathematically updated probability in the low 50s. While there is upside momentum from recent macro developments and pro-crypto sentiment in the US administration, this is largely historically priced in. Expecting a probability much higher than the mid-50s underestimates Bitcoin's historic tendency for deep double-digit percent pullbacks. I am forecasting 55% to reflect the positive price buffer we currently enjoy, tempered by the heavy statistical variance over the remaining of the year.
